channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Proposed New Zealand Immigration Bill Signals Shift from Humanitarian to Enforcement Focus
item.description: New Zealand’s proposed Immigration (Enhanced Risk Management) Amendment Bill has raised concerns that the country is moving away from its traditional humanitarian approach to asylum. The bill emphasis...

item.title: Ashley MacIsaac files defamation lawsuit against Google over AI-generated misinformation
item.description: Cape Breton musician Ashley MacIsaac has initiated a civil lawsuit against Google, alleging defamation after an AI-generated summary falsely identified him as a sex offender. The controversy arose in ...

item.title: Islamabad youth found dead in Mardan, prime suspect apprehended
item.description: In a disturbing incident, the body of 30-year-old Farrukh Afzal, who was abducted from Islamabad’s F-6/1 area, has been recovered from Mardan. Police stated that the victim appeared to have been tortu...

item.title: Understanding the Word of the Day: 'tabalu' on May 5, 2026
item.description: ABS-CBN Lifestyle features a Word of the Day for May 5, 2026, highlighting the term 'tabalu.' This initiative aims to enrich the Filipino vocabulary by introducing unique and interesting words regular...

item.title: Iranian Missile Strikes Target UAE Despite Ongoing Ceasefire
item.description: On May 4, 2026, the United Arab Emirates experienced its first attacks from Iran in nearly a month, challenging the fragile ceasefire that began around April 8 between Tehran and the U.S.-Israel allia...

item.title: DJ Krowbar Remembers Wife with Final Family Photo in Hospital
item.description: Kenyan gospel DJ and audio engineer Fred Karumba, popularly known as DJ Krowbar, has shared a deeply emotional moment from his family life. On May 4, 2026, he posted a photo capturing the last time hi...

item.title: Arteta Prepares Arsenal for Champions League Semi-Final Against Atletico Madrid
item.description: Mikel Arteta has emphasised that Arsenal are prepared to give their all in the Champions League semi-final second leg against Atletico Madrid. With the tie currently level at 1-1, the Gunners are one ...

item.title: Kwanza MP Kevin Wanyonyi Returns to Kenya After Five-Month Medical Treatment in India
item.description: Kwanza Constituency MP Kevin Ferdinand Wanyonyi has finally returned to Kenya after spending five months in India for specialised medical treatment. Initially, he had been receiving care at Nairobi Ho...

item.title: OpenAI President Greg Brockman Discusses Personal Wealth During Musk Trial
item.description: During the trial involving Elon Musk and OpenAI, the company's president, Greg Brockman, was required to explain diary entries that suggested a strong focus on financial gain. The entries, dating back...

item.title: President Ruto Clarifies Tanga Oil Refinery Plans Amid Regional Cooperation Talks
item.description: President William Ruto of Kenya has clarified his announcement about constructing an oil refinery in Tanga, Tanzania, following concerns raised by Tanzanian President Samia Suluhu Hassan who said she ...

item.title: UMC Mobile Stroke Unit Promotes Stroke Awareness Across El Paso
item.description: UMC El Paso is actively promoting stroke awareness throughout the city during May, which is recognized as Stroke Awareness Month. To educate the public and provide critical early treatment, the hospit...

item.title: Bad Bunny Debuts Realistic Aged Look at 2026 Met Gala
item.description: Puerto Rican superstar Bad Bunny, born Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, stunned attendees at the 2026 Met Gala with a striking and unusual appearance. For the event, the singer showcased a meticulously...

item.title: Explained: How Everton’s Thierno Barry Scored Despite Initial Offside Call
item.description: Everton pulled off a surprising 3-3 draw against Manchester City at the Hill Dickinson Stadium, causing a significant dent in City’s Premier League title hopes. The match saw Manchester City dominate ...

item.title: Prime Minister addresses rising attacks on British Jewish communities
item.description: The Prime Minister has commented on the recent surge in attacks against Jewish communities in the United Kingdom, describing the situation as a crisis that concerns the entire nation. While specific d...

item.title: Celebrities showcase bold styles at the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: The Met Gala, widely regarded as fashion’s most prestigious annual event, once again gathered some of the world’s biggest celebrities, designers, and cultural figures for a night of glamour and creati...

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni Settle Lawsuits Related to ‘It Ends With Us’ Dispute
item.description: Actors Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni have reached a settlement that ends their long-running legal dispute tied to the 2024 film “It Ends With Us.” The agreement was confirmed through a joint stateme...

item.title: BJP Considers Suvendu Adhikari, Samik Bhattacharya, and Agnimitra Paul for West Bengal Chief Minister Post
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) achieved a historic victory in the 2026 West Bengal assembly elections, winning 206 out of 293 contested seats, marking the first time the party has taken control in t...

item.title: Central Alberta authorities confirm 21-year-old's death as homicide
item.description: Authorities in central Alberta have officially classified the death of a 21-year-old as a homicide. While details about the circumstances surrounding the incident have not been fully released, local l...

item.title: Supreme Court Confirms Death Sentences for Chotu Gang Members in Rajanpur
item.description: ISLAMABAD: On Monday, the Supreme Court of Pakistan upheld the death sentences against members of the notorious Chotu gang, rejecting appeals filed by the gang leader Ghulam Rasool alias Chotu, along ...

item.title: Queensland Rail reduces services amid ongoing industrial dispute
item.description: Rail services across Brisbane are facing major disruptions due to an ongoing industrial dispute involving seven unions, including the Electrical Trades Union and the Rail, Tram and Bus Union. Queensla...
